steps.title: List the roots
steps.content:
Use each factor of $x^2(2x-13)(2x-u)=0$. The roots are
$$
0,\;0,\;\frac{13}{2},\;\frac{u}{2}
$$

steps.title: Set up the sum
steps.content:
The sum of the roots is given as $18$.
$$
0+0+\frac{13}{2}+\frac{u}{2}=18
$$
$$
\frac{13+u}{2}=18
$$

steps.title: Solve for u
steps.content: Multiply both sides by $2$, then subtract $13$. $$ 13+u=36 $$ $$ u=23 $$ The answer is 23.
